title: Roldana mezquitalana
intro: Roldana mezquitalana, also known as Mezquitil or Roldana, is a medicinal plant belonging to the Asteraceae family, traditionally used in Mexican folk medicine. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various ailments such as wounds, infections, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Roldana mezquitalana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Roldana mezquitalana is used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in herbal medicine to treat digestive problems, fever, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Roldana mezquitalana is classified in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family is one of the largest and most widespread in the plant kingdom, consisting of over 23,000 species. Roldana mezquitalana belongs to the genus Roldana, which is a group of flowering plants native to Mexico and Central America. The genus is further divided into several species, with Roldana mezquitalana being a unique and distinctive member. Roldana mezquitalana's classification also includes its order, family, and kingdom, which are Asterales, Asteraceae, and Plantae, respectively.
